commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8b7b3f746db7e771a92d7697c7116e0658e57a77)
commands.review_summary:
This PR routes post-tool raw reasoning `rawResponseItem/completed` notifications through the Codex post-tool continuation timeout, adds reasoning and non-reasoning timeout regressions, and updates Codex harness docs.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +111, Docs +3. Total +118 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and the focused regression shape: current main arms the short completion-idle watch for post-tool raw reasoning completions, while the PR test models the stale-session sequence. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review is still needed because the remaining blocker is direct real-runtime proof for the contributor's environment plus acceptance of the longer timeout tradeoff; automation can fix the docs paragraph but cannot supply the missing live proof.

target.title: [codex] Keep Codex waiting after raw reasoning progress
target.body:
## Summary
- Treat raw reasoning completion after a tool handoff as progress instead of a short completion-idle stall signal.
- Reuse the existing post-progress reply guard so long-running reasoning can continue until Codex sends a real terminal turn notification.
- Update the Codex turn-watch regression test to reproduce the stale-session sequence and prove it survives past the short guard.

## Root cause
A chilisaus task stalled because the embedded Codex app-server retired the turn after 60s of no stream events. Loki showed the last activity was rawResponseItem/completed with item type reasoning. The current OpenClaw logic armed the short completion-idle watch for that raw reasoning completion, so reasoning silence could be misclassified as a terminal stall.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Long-running Codex/chilisaus turns could be retired after 60 seconds when the last app-server notification was raw reasoning progress, leaving the delivery watcher alive but the underlying agent session stalled.
- Real environment tested: Alex's mac-mini OpenClaw production setup, Telegram-triggered chilisaus dispatch, Loki/OTel logs, and the local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Queried Loki/OTel for the failed production run, observed the scheduler-side watchdog fail the stale run after the watcher fix, then ran the patched OpenClaw Codex turn-watch path locally with the focused command shown under validation.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied live output from the real OpenClaw Telegram/scheduler run after the watchdog safety net was active:

  ```text
  Scheduler | chilisaus-deliver:build-soccer-betting-model | 59s ago
  ❌ chilisaus [build-soccer-betting-model] failed
  Error: agent session stalled: no session/jsonl activity for ~77min while delivery watcher remained alive; likely app-server turn retired or stopped producing events
  ```

  Redacted Loki/OTel trace excerpt from the same real incident before this OpenClaw patch identified the app-server root cause:

  ```text
  2026-06-01 20:08:46 EDT codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for completion
  idleMs=60000 timeoutMs=60000
  lastActivityReason=notification:rawResponseItem/completed
  lastNotificationMethod=rawResponseItem/completed
  lastNotificationItemType=reasoning
  reason=turn_completion_idle_timeout
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The scheduler no longer silently reports the stale job as running; it surfaces a hard stalled-session failure. This PR addresses the upstream OpenClaw side by keeping raw reasoning progress on the longer post-progress guard instead of the short 60s completion-idle path that caused that Loki trace.
- What was not tested: The patched OpenClaw source was not deployed/restarted on the production gateway in this PR run.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The production gateway was not restarted from this session, so the live runtime proof shows the real incident and safety-net behavior; the OpenClaw app-server behavior change is validated in the focused local regression below.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The Loki/OTel trace excerpt above is the before evidence for the app-server root cause.

## Validation
- pnpm test -- extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts
- Result: 1 test file passed, 55 tests passed
target.author: amittell

commands.author: amittell

target.title: Propagate ClickClack tool policy through reply dispatch
target.body:
## Summary
- Propagates ClickClack account tool policy through inbound agent reply dispatch
- Threads runtime tool restrictions through shared reply dispatch into agent execution

## Changes
- Adds a runtime `toolsAllow` handoff on shared inbound dispatch params
- Carries the policy through reply options into embedded and CLI agent execution
- Adds regression coverage for ClickClack dispatch, shared dispatch, and embedded agent execution

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: ClickClack account-level tool allowlists are carried into the agent reply runtime so the account policy remains the effective runtime tool set.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw worktree `~/Worktrees/openclaw/openclaw/fix/fix-708` at commit `8a5cc59`, Node `v22.22.2`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a production embedded-agent tool-policy boundary probe with `node --import tsx` from the PR worktree after the patch.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied console output from the local OpenClaw run:

```text
[proof-708] command=node --import tsx <production embedded-agent tool-policy boundary>
[proof-708] runtimeToolsAllow=["message"]
[proof-708] constructionPlan={"constructTools":true,"includeCoreTools":true,"runtimeToolAllowlist":["message"],"codingToolConstructionPlan":{"includeBaseCodingTools":false,"includeShellTools":false,"includeChannelTools":false,"includeOpenClawTools":true,"includePluginTools":false}}
[proof-708] availableTools=["message","exec","read","web_search"]
[proof-708] allowedToolsAfterRuntimePolicy=["message"]
[proof-708] bundleMcpRuntimeCreated=false
[proof-708] result=pass
```

- Observed result after fix: The runtime allowlist was `["message"]`; `exec`, `read`, and `web_search` were excluded from the allowed tool set, and the broader bundle MCP runtime was not created.
- What was not tested: Live ClickClack service delivery and a real model-provider turn were not run locally; those require external service credentials.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The live proof is scoped to the production embedded-agent tool-policy boundary. Regression coverage separately exercises ClickClack dispatch propagation and shared reply dispatch propagation.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-clickclack.config.ts extensions/clickclack/src/inbound.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:extensions`
- `git diff --check`
- `claude -p "/review 89500"` returned `approve`

## Notes
- CLI-backed agent runs already fail closed when a runtime tool allowlist is requested, because that backend cannot enforce the runtime policy.
